name: Flipped SU(5)
summary: Variant of SU(5) GUT with the SM hypercharge embedded as a linear combination of SU(5)-internal U(1) and an additional external U(1)_X. The 'flipping' interchanges up- and down-type fermion roles in the SU(5) representations; each SM generation lives in (10, 1/2) + (5-bar, -3/2) + (1, 5/2) of SU(5) x U(1)_X. Naturally arises in heterotic-string compactifications and avoids the doublet-triplet splitting problem of minimal SU(5).

addresses_puzzles.note: Gauge couplings unify at M_GUT ~ 10^16 GeV with SUSY content; the U(1)_X factor adds flexibility relative to minimal SU(5).
Right-handed neutrinos sit naturally in the 10 + 5-bar; type-I seesaw at intermediate scales follows.

touches_benchmarks.note: Flipped SU(5) suppresses the dominant p -> e+ pi^0 channel by group-theoretic factors; predicts longer lifetimes than minimal SUSY-SU(5) and thus easier consistency with Super-K bounds.

introduces.note: Two gauge couplings, GUT-Higgs sector, intermediate breaking scales.

history: Barr (1982) introduced flipped SU(5). Antoniadis, Ellis, Hagelin, Nanopoulos (1987), 'Supersymmetric flipped SU(5) revitalized', Phys. Lett. B 194, 231 — modern supersymmetric formulation. Modern revival as a string-inspired GUT alternative; specific predictions for proton decay channels distinguish from minimal SU(5).

references.citation: Antoniadis, Ellis, Hagelin, Nanopoulos, 'Supersymmetric flipped SU(5) revitalized', Phys. Lett. B 194, 231 (1987)
